title: Generics, Type Sets, Constraints, Inference, and Iteration · Contract Triage
summary: A collection library needs reusable operations without reflection, lost relationships, over-broad constraints, or unreadable APIs. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a constrained reusable collection library and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Define generic functions and types, follow type inference, and retain relationships among parameters and results.
Build minimal constraints from method elements, unions, underlying-type approximations, comparable, and intersection semantics.
Use only operations guaranteed by the type set and explain why fields, methods, conversions, and nil differ across type arguments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
